episode_meta.title: Aliya Itzkowitz and Sam Gould (Part Two) Potential of Multimodal AI and Autonomous AI Agents in Publishing

use_cases.description: Using image-to-text models to generate captions for images in articles
use_cases.outcome: Automated generation of image descriptions for editorial content

use_cases.description: Translating foreign language text within images (e.g., street signs, protest placards) to help tell multilingual stories
use_cases.outcome: Breaking language barriers in visual journalism

use_cases.description: Converting wireframe designs and Figma designs into working website code
use_cases.outcome: Significantly speeding up design-to-code development process

use_cases.description: Autonomous agent that scrapes competitor pricing from websites by taking screenshots, interpreting pages via vision model, and navigating to extract data
use_cases.tool_used: GPT-4 Vision with autonomous agent framework
use_cases.outcome: Created in 15 minutes; runs in 5 minutes to collect competitor pricing data that would be very hard to do at scale traditionally

use_cases.description: Multiple AI agents simulating different roles of a software company (CEO, CTO, coder) collaborating to produce working code
use_cases.outcome: Demonstrated that complex coding tasks can be broken down and handled by multiple cooperating agents

use_cases.description: Personalization of content formats for readers including summarization, re-wording articles into personalized tones, and providing more context behind stories
use_cases.outcome: Getting quality journalism in front of more eyes by tailoring delivery

use_cases.description: Internal administrative use cases such as staffing projects, budgeting, and other business operations using AI agents
use_cases.outcome: Potentially huge efficiencies for internal-facing tasks

use_cases.description: Using ChatGPT for writing code as part of personal and professional workflow
use_cases.outcome: Found LLMs much more effective for structured code than for blog writing

use_cases.description: Building an automated morning motivational message system that picks a random principle, sends it to an LLM, and texts the result each morning
use_cases.outcome: Daily personalized motivational texts generated from a database of principles

use_cases.description: Using ChatGPT to compose poems for friends' birthdays, colleagues' send-offs, and similar occasions
use_cases.outcome: Fun creative output, though requiring modification to tone down ChatGPT-style adjectives

use_cases.description: Creating an AI Santa character for an internal Christmas email using image generation
Using Dataminr for hyper-local news filtering and personal information advantage
use_cases.outcome: Filtered hyper-local news alerts (collisions, helicopter activity, etc.)

key_claims.claim: 2023 was all about text generation tools like ChatGPT, but 2024 will be all about multimodal AI
key_claims.speaker: Nikita Roy

key_claims.claim: Multimodal models like image-to-text are not in the forefront of everyone's consciousness; people are still trying to get to grips with text-to-text models, creating a real opportunity for businesses to move quickly and get ahead of the curve
key_claims.speaker: Sam Gould

key_claims.claim: People only care that something is AI when the AI doesn't work or does something wrong
key_claims.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

key_claims.claim: As more news publishers start working with AI, the advantages will get competed away just like websites and apps did, so it always comes back to offering truly unique and differentiated content
key_claims.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

key_claims.claim: Bill Gates wrote that AI agents are the future of how everyone will interact with software
key_claims.speaker: Sam Gould

key_claims.claim: The destination of the future for publishers may look less like a website and more like a custom agent
key_claims.speaker: Sam Gould

key_claims.claim: LLMs are much more effective for writing code than for writing blog articles due to the structured nature of code
key_claims.speaker: Sam Gould

key_claims.claim: By the time you think through your prompt and clear instructions for blog writing, you've already worked out what you're trying to say without needing the LLM output
key_claims.speaker: Sam Gould

key_claims.claim: There tends to be a six-month or one-year lag before innovation case studies get written up by official trade bodies
key_claims.speaker: Sam Gould

challenges.description: Image-to-text models can go wrong in many ways when generating captions (as documented by Simon Willison's article on GPT-4V and prompt injection)
News industry has historically been a one-size-fits-all model for user experience, making it difficult to differentiate through UI/UX
AI agent systems are quite complex and may be too early for the industry to fully adopt
Web scraping at scale is very hard because web pages have all different formats, making it difficult to create reliable scraping code

Separating signal from noise in AI information: there is a lot of promotional fluff on sources like LinkedIn, and it is difficult for people not well-versed in the field to distinguish valuable content from advertisements
ChatGPT-generated text has recognizable tropes (excessive enthusiastic adjectives) that require human modification to be usable
Internal-facing AI use cases (administrative functions, staffing, budgeting) don't get as much airtime as newsroom use cases, leading people to overlook potentially huge efficiencies
The industry may overestimate readers' desire for things to be human-led or human-created

quotes.text: I think people only care that it's AI when the AI doesn't work or does something wrong
quotes.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz (paraphrasing a conference attendee)
quotes.topic: Reader perception of AI in news

quotes.text: I think it always comes back to the same thing in our industry, which is are you offering people content that's truly unique and differentiated or not? And that's always at the heart of most of the problems that publications are facing, whether it's how do I monetize, how do I transform, how do I survive
quotes.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz
quotes.topic: Content differentiation as the core challenge for publishers

quotes.text: The key way that we saw that being used was not so much as a generation tool, but really to look at it as kind of an interpretation tool. So it's not about maybe how people are conceptualizing language models and text-to-text models as giving an input and then trying to generate lots and lots of text content... But really we wanted to look at models like GPT for vision and image to text models as interpreting the image and providing some rich information back
quotes.speaker: Sam Gould
quotes.topic: Reframing multimodal AI as interpretation rather than generation

quotes.text: This is really a call to action for the industry to say you have to think about this because... there is this sense of maybe of one size fits all, there is this also this opportunity to innovate in this area. One could argue that readers and audiences are starting to look for this
quotes.speaker: Sam Gould

quotes.text: A destination of the future starts to look less like a website and more like a custom agent
quotes.speaker: Sam Gould
quotes.topic: AI agents as the future of publisher platforms

quotes.text: You should work out kind of what you're looking to get. Are you looking for practical code snippets and tutorials? Are you looking for thought leadership? Like what would be most useful? And then to try and pick the sources and the people and curate your feeds to really support that so that you can separate out the signal from the noise
quotes.speaker: Sam Gould

recommendations.advice: Think of multimodal AI as an interpretation tool rather than a generation tool — use image-to-text models to extract and interpret information from images rather than trying to generate new content
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould

recommendations.advice: Start thinking about how to become a destination rather than just a source — consider how AI can enable richer, more interactive user experiences
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould

recommendations.advice: Don't overlook internal-facing AI use cases (administrative tasks, staffing, budgeting) which may offer more immediate and actionable efficiencies than newsroom-facing ones
recommendations.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

recommendations.advice: Put AI agents on your research radar now, even if it may seem too soon — other industries are already building with these tools and the technology will only get easier to work with
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould

recommendations.advice: Cultivate a fluff detector when consuming AI information — learn to separate promotional content from genuinely valuable insights, and curate your information sources based on what you specifically need (practical tutorials vs. thought leadership)
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould
recommendations.target_audience: Anyone keeping up with AI developments

recommendations.advice: Follow specific people rather than topics for staying current — identify thought leaders on LinkedIn, Medium, Twitter, and technical sources like GitHub and Reddit based on what type of content they produce
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould and Aliya Itzkowitz

recommendations.advice: Read Simon Willison's article on GPT-4V and prompt injection before deploying vision models for tasks like image captioning
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould
recommendations.target_audience: Developers and publishers implementing multimodal AI

recommendations.advice: Focus on content quality — scoops, angles, and on-the-ground reporting — as the primary differentiator, with AI supporting delivery and personalization of that journalism
recommendations.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

external_references: Simon Willison's article/blog on GPT-4V and prompt injection
Bill Gates article on AI agents as the future of software interaction
PAIR (Google's AI UX lab) — working on guidelines for news publications on responsible AI in user experiences
ChatDev (multi-agent research framework simulating software company roles)
FT Strategies Insights page and newsletter
New York Times strategy on becoming a 'destination'
arXiv (paper repository, mentioned as having wrapper tools for custom feeds)
